description: Assist with weekly lab sessions: walk students through Git branching, Maven or manual builds, and JUnit tests; answer questions on requirements and Agile iteration planning.
requirements: Strong Java; comfortable with Git and JUnit; able to explain basic UML and user stories in English.

description: Grade programming assignments (lists, trees, graphs) and short complexity questions; follow the rubric and return feedback within 5 working days.
requirements: Python or C++; solid grasp of big-O, BSTs, heaps, and basic graph algorithms.

description: Support lab exercises on processes, threads, and simple memory models; help students use POSIX APIs in the Linux VM and submit lab reports on time.
requirements: C or Java; basic Linux shell; understanding of synchronization and deadlocks.

description: Run tutorial sessions on socket programming and packet analysis with Wireshark; clarify TCP vs UDP and basic routing concepts.
requirements: Networking basics; Python or Java for small socket demos; good spoken English.

description: Help in drop-in labs with IDE setup, syntax errors, and simple debugging for first-year students (loops, arrays, functions).
requirements: Patient communication; Python or Java introductory level; prior tutoring experience is a plus.
